name: bare-closing
note: First fixture of the twin conformance corpus (2026-07-16, bare-closing conformance port). NEUTRAL GROUND: this file belongs to neither twin's directory; both scher/src/society.ts (vitest: test/conformance.bare-closing.test.ts) and scher/scher-core/src/lib.rs (cargo: tests/bare_closing_fixture.rs) replay these SAME rows and must produce these SAME readings, or the engines have diverged. Rows are laid verbatim via the one write (lay), including the ~q mode-beats — no layP/lay_p constructor, so both harnesses stay byte-symmetric and guard-free. The scenario: a story unpacked into its three poles (End AND Now, both structurally designated), one charge laid and witnessed by the story's own frame, then closed by a BARE edge out of the End (the 2026-07-15 bare-closing ruling: edge direction alone carries the meaning; no q-grounding marker). UPDATED 2026-07-20 (two same-day rulings): (1) charge-direction — 'the End prehends the capture' — charge edges are now subject=End, object=charged-event (was the reverse); (2) story-designate-now-poles — since a charge and a closing are now BOTH bare edges FROM the End, a q-now-pole designation on the story's own Now is what tells them apart (a closing's object is a designated now-pole; a charge's is not). Expected reads pin end_actual/endActual, voltage_of/voltageOf, and the reaches walk THROUGH the bare closing, at asOf moments before and after each lay.

rows.content: the End-pole of task, not yet actual
the Now of task's own frame
the story's frame witnesses its charge
rows.subject: task~now

rows.content: the story's frame witnesses its charge [q-grounding]
rows.subject: task~now~because~task~hea~charge-0

rows.content: the end is because now (frame: task)
rows.subject: task~hea
